reviews.review_description: The movement is complicated, unintuitive and way too slow: stop assuming everyone is a VR baby and cant handle basic locomotion these days. Children use these devices. The interface for changing tools is unintuitive as well, constantly requiring checking your hands to see what tool you accidentally selected. The preincluded assets are extremely basic and i couldnt find at a glance how to import more from my file system. Export is buried under the start menu when it should be included inside the project, not a single item away from deletion. The texturing system is outdated in design and extremely clunky, it broke all my colors when i changed it to 'fastest' mode and i couldnt get it to set a white object color. It takes forever to size up large items and requires manually moving yourself to the edge of it to do so. Theres no button to quickly return to the center, teleportation isnt very functional when dealing with large environment builds. The wireframes for scale and rotation are tiny and impossible to use at scale.. all around, I regret spending money on this app, and I wouldnt recommend it over any number of better, often free alternatives to asset design for anyone who is serious about this skill. I can only hope these frictions will be sorted out in time and i can adjust my review later, because this looks and feels like 1970s software running on a 2030s device. This is an exact example of why i dont buy software often.

reviews.review_title: VR Model Making with powerful tools.
reviews.review_description:
In the 1990s a talented coder created a software application called Anim8or. Still there when I looked. Still excellent too.
This afforded high end modelling techniques to amateurs and gave you an excellent grounding in the language, methodologies and techniques used by professionals. You could then take your rendered models into an in-app film studio and animate fly-bys and other complex motions. It gave you a chance to emulate the big-guns current in the CGI universe at the time. 

This gem of an app does exactly the same and in a VR environment.

It encourages exploration , trial and error and practice.
There is solid ground work for beginners in here. A really excellent place to start to explore the craft and imagination of Subdivisional Node Based Polygonal Modelling and more.
It’s a skill and tricky stuff to master but to have an app of this quality to “cut your chops” on is great.

It’s like being back in 1999 but in 2022 . Exciting times.

An animation studio plug-in would be welcome as would more varied in-built textures.

Check out , online , the Ani8or galleries of models for inspiration.
Also check out Arkio in App Lab. They are also moving this type of work forward in exciting ways.

This is how the next generation of superstars get started. 

Fab!
